elements.title: Talbot Street Baptist Church
elements.contents: ... that Talbot Street Baptist Church (pictured) was painted red, which was the style at the time, only to have its original polychrome brickwork restored later?

elements.title: Camila Ashland
elements.contents: ... that a case of mistaken identity made actress Camila Ashland the target of a flood of protesting phone calls?

elements.title: 1935 Kentucky State Thorobreds
elements.contents: ... that the 1935 Kentucky State Thorobreds won a black college football national championship with "Tarzan" playing quarterback?

elements.title: William Henry Sleeman
elements.contents: ... that the letters of British colonial administrator William Henry Sleeman were posthumously published in 1858 to exonerate him during the Indian Rebellion?

elements.title: Begged
elements.contents: ... that Olivia Rodrigo secretly premiered her song "Begged" at an invite-only show with a no-phones policy?

elements.title: Rodolfo da Ponte
elements.contents: ... that fencer Rodolfo da Ponte was selected to compete for Paraguay at the 1968 Summer Olympics after the rowing and football teams did not qualify?

elements.title: his newspaper
elements.contents: ... that US president Warren G. Harding died less than two months after selling his newspaper?

elements.title: Elsie Eiler
elements.contents: ... that Elsie Eiler, the only resident of Monowi, Nebraska, is also the city's mayor, tavernkeeper, and librarian?

elements.title: a new genus of wasps
elements.contents: ... that David Attenborough's 100th birthday was marked by the naming of a new genus of wasps after him and the announcement that he would narrate Blue Planet III?
